exercises.name: Rudiments with Metronome (all 40)
exercises.how: Practice rudiments to a click at specific note values, increasing tempo only when perfect.

exercises.name: Clapping/Tapping Rhythm Along to Music
exercises.how: Tap or clap the beat and mimic rhythmic patterns from recordings, from slow to complex.

exercises.name: Long Tones with Tuner/Drone
exercises.develops: Pitch stability and centering on each note
exercises.also: Knowledge of instrument's pitch tendencies, embouchure/air consistency
exercises.how: Sustain each note against a tuner or drone, learning which notes run sharp/flat and how to adjust.

exercises.name: Slow Scales with Drone
exercises.how: Play scales slowly against a tonic drone, tuning every degree by ear.

exercises.name: Overtone Series Studies
exercises.develops: Fine mouth/oral-cavity and air control; harmonic awareness
exercises.also: Range, resonance, voicing (esp. flute harmonics & sax overtones)
exercises.how: Produce upper partials on a fixed fingering; on flute/sax, overblowing low fingerings to sound harmonics trains voicing and air speed.

exercises.name: Long Tones
exercises.develops: Breath support; steady airstream; tone consistency
exercises.also: Pitch stability, dynamic control, embouchure endurance, intonation awareness
exercises.how: Hold a single note as long as possible with steady pitch and volume; increase duration and vary dynamics over time.

exercises.name: Diaphragmatic (Belly) Breathing
exercises.also: Relaxation, sustained phrases, reduced chest/throat tension
exercises.how: Hand on belly, hand on chest; inhale so only the belly rises. Trains efficient, low breathing.

exercises.name: Straw Breathing / Pursed-Lip Resistance
exercises.how: Inhale/exhale slowly through a straw or pursed lips; the resistance forces steady regulation of air pressure.
